action: Create a fail-closed startup adapter that verifies the commit-bound effective queue and returns exactly one highest-priority actionable item owned by the requesting team.

observed: M-013 completed consumer-side artifact verification and handed off verified owned-item selection.
The new adapter verifies the artifact first, filters by exact owner and actionable status, resolves dependencies, and deterministically selects one item by priority and id.
Five synthetic adversarial tests and CI wiring are committed.
inferred: A runtime using this adapter will fail closed rather than consume stale, tampered, foreign-owner, or dependency-blocked work.
proposed: Add a thin team-start wrapper or shared invocation contract so every scheduled team calls the selector before reading its chosen work item.
superseded: Manual queue scanning as an adequate autonomous selection boundary.
Selecting solely by owner and priority without verifying provenance, status, and dependencies.
unresolved: GitHub Actions runtime success for the selector workflow is not yet proven.
Peer team prompts do not yet execute the adapter directly.

verification: Module, five-case synthetic test suite, CI path triggers, and fail-closed test command were committed and source-inspected. Runtime passage is not claimed without a workflow result.
privacy_boundary: Public repository metadata, abstract queue records, synthetic fixtures, and generated evidence only; private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads excluded.
handoff: Continuity Mining should next define a shared team-start invocation contract that maps each registered team to its exact owner id, invokes the selector, records the selection digest, and refuses implementation when no accepted item is returned.
